questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $D, E, F$ be points on the sides $B C, C A, A B$ of a triangle $A B C$, respectively. Suppose $A D, B E, C F$ are concurrent at $P$. If $P F / P C=2 / 3, P E / P B=2 / 7$ and $P D / P A=m / n$, where $m, n$ are positive integers with $\operatorname{gcd}(m, n)=1$, find $m+n$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \because \frac{P F}{P C}=\frac{2}{3} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{P F}{C F}=\frac{2}{5}=\frac{\Delta_1+\Delta_3}{\Delta}...(i) \end{aligned}$
Similarly $\quad \frac{2}{9}=\frac{\Delta_2+\Delta_6}{\Delta}$...(ii) and $\quad \frac{m}{m+n}=\frac{\Delta_4+\Delta_5}{\Delta}$...(iii) Adding (i), (ii) and (iii); we get $\begin{aligned} & \frac{2}{5}+\frac{2}{9}+\frac{m}{m+n}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{m}{m+n}=1-\frac{28}{45} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{m}{m+n}=\frac{17}{45} \\ & \Rightarrow 28 m=17 n \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{m}{n}=\frac{17}{28} \\ & \Rightarrow m+n=45 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In parallelogram $A B C D, A C=10$ and $B D=28$. The points $K$ and $L$ in the plane of $A B C D$ move in such a way that $A K=B D$ and $B L=A C$. Let $M$ and $N$ be the midpoints of $C K$ and $D L$, respectively. What is the maximum value of $\cot ^2(\angle \mathrm{BMD} / 2)+\tan ^2(\angle \mathrm{ANC} / 2) ?$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{CK}_1=18$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{OM}_1=5+9=14 \\ & \therefore \quad \angle \mathrm{BM}_1 \mathrm{D}=90^{\circ}(\angle \text { in semicircle }) \\ & \text { Similarly, } \mathrm{CK}_2=10+28=38 \\ & \therefore \quad \mathrm{CM}_2=19 \\ & \quad \mathrm{AM}_2=9 \\ & \quad \mathrm{OM}_2=5+9=14 \\ & \therefore \quad \angle \mathrm{BM}_2 \mathrm{D}=90^{\circ}(\angle \text { in semicircle }) \end{aligned}$ If $\mathrm{K}_1$ or $\mathrm{K}_2$ moves $\angle \mathrm{BMD}$ will increase By observation : $\frac{\angle \mathrm{BMD}}{2} \uparrow \quad \therefore \cot \left(\frac{\angle \mathrm{BMD}}{2}\right) \downarrow$ $\therefore \quad$ For maximum $\quad \angle \mathrm{BMD}=90^{\circ}$ $\cot ^2\left(\frac{\angle \mathrm{BMD}}{2}\right)=1$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{BL}_1=10, \mathrm{OL}_1=4, \mathrm{DL}_1=18, \mathrm{DN}_1=9, \mathrm{ON}_1=5 \\ & \therefore \angle \mathrm{AN}_1 \mathrm{C}=\frac{\pi}{2}(\angle \text { in semicircle }) \end{aligned}$ For L2 point, $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{DL}_2=14+14+10 \\ & \mathrm{DL}_2=38 \\ & \mathrm{DN}_2=19 \\ & \mathrm{ON}_2=5 \end{aligned}$ $\angle \mathrm{AN}_2 \mathrm{C}=\frac{\pi}{2}(\angle \text { in semicircle })$ Now if $L_1$ or $L_2$ moves, $\angle \mathrm{ANC} \downarrow \quad \frac{\angle \mathrm{ANC}}{2} \downarrow \quad \tan \frac{\angle \mathrm{ANC}}{2} \downarrow \text { (Observation) }$ Hence for maximum, $\begin{aligned} & \angle A N C=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & \therefore \quad \tan ^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=1 \\ & \therefore \quad \text { Maximum }=1+1=2 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: In a parallelogram $A B C D$, the point $P$ on a segment $A B$ is taken such that $\frac{A P}{A B}=\frac{61}{2022}$ and a point $Q$ on the segment $A D$ is taken such that $\frac{A Q}{A D}=\frac{61}{2065}$. If $P Q$ intersects $A C$ at $T$, find $\frac{A C}{A T}$ to the nearest integer.
questions.solution: Here, $\alpha=\frac{61}{2022}$ and $\beta=\frac{61}{2065}$ $\vec{t}=\frac{\vec{b}+\vec{d}}{1+\lambda}=\frac{\alpha \mu \vec{b}+\beta \vec{d}}{1+\mu}$ So, $\frac{1}{1+\lambda}=\frac{\alpha \mu}{1+\mu}$ and $\frac{1}{1+\lambda}=\frac{\beta}{1+\mu}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \alpha \mu=\beta \text { hence } \frac{1}{1+\lambda}=\frac{\beta}{1+\frac{\beta}{\alpha}} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 1+\lambda=\frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha \beta} \end{aligned}$ Clearly, $\begin{aligned} \frac{A C}{A T}=\frac{1+\lambda}{1} & =\frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha \beta}=\frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta} \\ & =\frac{2022}{61}+\frac{2065}{61} \\ & =\frac{4087}{61}=67\end{aligned}$
